skill.name: longbridge-fundamentals
skill.description:
Financial statements, business segments, dividends, valuation multiples (PE/PB/PS), industry comparison, operating data, corporate actions, company and executive profiles, cross-stock comparison, and valuation ranking via Longbridge. Also: DCF models, value investing screens (low PE/PB, margin of safety), and behavioral finance analysis frameworks.
Triggers: "财报", "三表", "利润表", "资产负债", "现金流", "估值", "PE", "PB", "分红", "公司信息", "高管", "行业估值", "并购", "DCF", "内在价值", "低估值", "安全边际", "行为金融", "小盘成长", "专精特新", "財報", "估值", "分紅", "內在價值", "安全邊際", "financial report", "income statement", "balance sheet", "valuation", "dividend", "company info", "industry valuation", "DCF", "value screen", "behavioral finance", "利潤表", "資產負債", "現金流", "行業估值", "併購", "行為金融", "小盤成長"
skill.summary: Access financial statements, valuation metrics, and company fundamentals for global markets via Longbridge CLI/MCP, with built-in DCF, value screen, and behavioral finance frameworks.
skill.author: longbridge

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only skill consisting of 1 SKILL.md and 20 markdown reference files. There are no executable scripts, no Python or shell code, and no network or filesystem operations. The static analyzer flagged 242 instances of 'backtick execution' which are all false positives — they are standard Markdown inline code formatting (e.g., `longbridge --help`, `AAPL.US`, `700.HK`). The 14 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings and 4 'System reconnaissance' findings are also false positives triggered by financial analysis terms (e.g., 'hashing' in the sense of data grouping, 'system' in the sense of market system) in pure documentation. No malicious code or dangerous patterns were found.

low_findings.title: False positive: backtick patterns in markdown
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 242 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' across all markdown files. These are all standard Markdown inline code formatting used to render CLI command names and stock symbols (e.g., `longbridge --help`, `AAPL.US`). No actual code execution occurs.

low_findings.title: False positive: weak cryptographic algorithm terms in documentation
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged mentions of 'hashing' and similar terms in financial analysis context. The references files discuss data bucketing (e.g., 'hashing peers into buckets' for percentile ranking) and financial terminology, not actual cryptographic operations.

low_findings.title: False positive: system reconnaissance terms in financial docs
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged words like 'system' and 'reconnaissance' in financial analysis context. These refer to market systems and industry reconnaissance, not system-level access.

content.user_title: Analyze stock fundamentals with Longbridge data
content.value_statement: Access comprehensive financial data and valuation analysis for global stocks without manual research. This skill orchestrates Longbridge CLI and MCP tools to deliver financial statements, valuation metrics, and company insights in seconds.
actual_capabilities: Fetch income statements, balance sheets, and cash flow statements for any global stock
Retrieve valuation metrics including PE, PB, PS, EV/EBITDA, and dividend yield with historical percentiles
Compare a stock against industry peers with premium/discount and percentile rank analysis
Access business segment breakdowns, dividend history, and corporate action records
Build DCF intrinsic value models using historical free cash flow and WACC inputs
Screen stock universes for value candidates using low PE/PB, high ROE, and dividend yield filters
limitations: Operating review data is only available for Hong Kong-listed stocks
Historical valuation percentile analysis requires at least 1 year of price history
Industry comparisons degrade when fewer than 5 peers exist in the same industry
All output is for reference only and does not constitute investment advice

use_cases.title: Quick company snapshot for retail investors
use_cases.description: Get a complete picture of a company's financials, valuation, and key executives in one query without switching between multiple platforms.

use_cases.title: DCF and intrinsic value modeling for analysts
use_cases.description: Build discounted cash flow models with historical FCF, WACC, and terminal value calculations to estimate intrinsic value versus current market price.

use_cases.title: Value stock screening across markets
use_cases.description: Screen index constituents like CSI 300, HSI, or S&P 500 for undervalued candidates using composite value scores based on PE, PB, ROE, and yield.

prompt_templates.title: Get a company financial summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the latest financial statements and key valuation metrics for NVDA.US

prompt_templates.title: Compare valuation with industry peers
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare the PE, PB, and PS ratios of 700.HK against its industry peers. Is it trading at a premium or discount?

prompt_templates.title: Run a DCF intrinsic value analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a DCF model for AAPL.US using the last 5 years of free cash flow, a 9% WACC, and a 3% terminal growth rate. What is the intrinsic value per share?

prompt_templates.title: Screen for value stocks in an index
prompt_templates.prompt: Screen the CSI 300 (000300.SH) for stocks with PE under 20, PB under 2, and ROE above 10%. Show the top 10 candidates ranked by composite value score.

output_examples.input: Show me the financial statements and valuation for NVDA.US

output: Overall: Trading at a moderate discount to industry median

best_practices: Always run `longbridge --help` first to discover available subcommands and their current flags before calling them
Use concurrent CLI calls for multi-dimensional analysis to reduce total response time
Include the data source attribution and not-investment-advice disclaimer in all output
Handle sparse data gracefully by degrading to snapshot-only when historical or peer data is insufficient
anti_patterns: Do not hard-code specific subcommand names or flag values in queries — the CLI evolves and hard-coded names will become stale
Do not use this skill to generate real-time trading signals or automated buy/sell recommendations
Do not treat a single valuation metric (e.g., low PE) in isolation — always consider the industry cycle, business quality, and historical context

faq.question: Does this skill require a Longbridge account?
faq.answer: No. All data commands used by this skill are public and do not require login. Only trading-related operations need authentication.

faq.question: Which markets are supported?
faq.answer: Hong Kong, US (NYSE/NASDAQ), A-shares (Shanghai/Shenzhen), and Singapore exchanges are supported. Operating review data is only available for HK stocks.

faq.question: How do I switch between the CLI and MCP?
faq.answer: The skill uses the Longbridge CLI by default. If the CLI is not installed, it falls back to the MCP server. Run `longbridge auth login` if authentication is needed for MCP.

faq.question: What languages does the output support?
faq.answer: The skill detects your input language and replies in Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, or English. Numeric values remain unchanged across languages.

faq.question: Can I use this for automated trading?
faq.answer: No. This skill is for research and analysis only. It does not execute trades, and all output explicitly disclaims investment advice. Use Longbridge's trading platform for order execution.

faq.question: What happens if valuation data is missing?
faq.answer: The skill degrades gracefully. If historical data is under 1 year, it shows only the current snapshot. If fewer than 5 peers exist, it shows a relative comparison but notes the sample is sparse.
